name: Villamediana from Valdeolmillos
name_original: Villamediana desde Valdeolmillos — circular por el Monte Rey
description: Cycle a moderate 26.6-mile route from Valdeolmillos, gaining 1137 feet, exploring Palencia's charming villages and open countryside.
region_intro: Road cycling around Valdeolmillos, located in the Cerrato Palentino region of Palencia province, Spain, offers varied terrain for cyclists. The area is characterized by rolling hills and valleys, providing diverse routes from challenging climbs to more relaxed stretches. Cyclists can explore a network of low-traffic roads, allowing for tranquil rides through the undulating countryside. This region is actively promoting cycling tourism, integrating cultural attractions and historical sites into its routes. Best road cycling routes around Valdeolmillos The most popular road cycling route is Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo, a 66.9 miles (107.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects historical sites and offers extensive views across the Palencia landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villamediana from Valdeolmillos, a moderate 26.6 miles (42.8 km) path. This route starts directly from Valdeolmillos and traverses the region's characteristic rolling hills. Local road cyclists also love the Magaz and Baltanás El Cerrato Loop, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail leading through the heart of the Cerrato region, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Valdeolmillos is defined by rolling hills, valleys, and low-traffic roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Valdeolmillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdeolmillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valdeolmillos?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Valdeolmillos, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy to difficult, with the majority being moderate.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Valdeolmillos?
A: The region around Valdeolmillos, part of the Cerrato Palentino, is characterized by rolling hills and valleys. This varied topography provides diverse routes, from challenging climbs to more relaxed stretches through undulating countryside. You'll primarily ride on a network of well-preserved, low-traffic roads, offering a tranquil environment.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Valdeolmillos?
A: Yes, Valdeolmillos offers several routes suitable for beginners or families. There are 7 easy routes available, providing a gentle introduction to road cycling in the area. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What are some longer road cycling routes for experienced riders in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking longer distances, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158326535/fromista-from-palencia-circular-by-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km and connects historical sites. While most routes are moderate, their length and cumulative elevation can provide a good challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Valdeolmillos?
A: Yes, you can find circular routes in the area. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243308538/villamediana-from-valdeolmillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamediana from Valdeolmillos&lt;/a&gt; route starts directly from the village and offers a moderate loop through the characteristic rolling hills. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170653750/magaz-and-baltanas-el-cerrato-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaz and Baltanás El Cerrato Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the heart of the Cerrato region.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or cultural attractions can I discover along road cycling routes in the Cerrato Palentino region?
A: The Cerrato Palentino region integrates cultural attractions into its cycling routes. You can explore historical sites like the Church of San Juan Bautista in Valdeolmillos or visit the unique 'barrios bodegas' (underground wineries). Routes often pass through charming towns where you might find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Palencia&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5793615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Puentecillas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Valdeolmillos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil, low-traffic roads and the varied, scenic landscape of rolling hills and valleys that make for an engaging ride.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Valdeolmillos?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region's climate suggests spring and autumn are ideal for road biking. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring the rolling hills and cultural sites without the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or places to rest along the road cycling routes?
A: The Cerrato Palentino region is actively promoting cycling tourism, which often includes support for amenities. While specific cafes aren't listed, routes frequently pass through villages and towns where you can expect to find local establishments for refreshments and rest, especially in larger towns like Palencia or Astudillo.

Q: Is there parking available for road cyclists in Valdeolmillos?
A: Valdeolmillos is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, making it a convenient starting point for many routes. As the region promotes cycling, you'll typically find suitable places to park your vehicle before heading out on your ride.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring historical sites in the region?
A: Absolutely. The Palencia Provincial Council is investing in cycling routes that connect the main tourist attractions of the Cerrato region, often linked to local culture and history. Many routes will take you past historical churches, traditional villages, and even unique 'bodegas del Cerrato' (underground wineries), allowing for a rich cultural experience alongside your ride.
tips: Surrounded by a landscape of cereal fields is the small town of Valdeolmillos. The view of the bell tower of the Church of San Juan Bautista will guide your way as it towers over the city.

Riding northeast of the capital of Palencia, the town of Villamediana surrounds a landscape of valleys covered with holm oaks. The town has a patrimonial medieval architecture being the Church of Santa Columba the most important monument.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Villamediana from Valdeolmillos route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Valdeolmillos. You can typically find parking within the village itself, near the starting point of the loop. As it's a rural area, street parking is usually available.

faqs.question: Is the Villamediana from Valdeolmillos route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash on roads can be unsafe for both the cyclist and the dog. It's best to leave your canine companion at home for this specific type of cycling tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Palencia province generally offers good cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder weather. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Villamediana from Valdeolmillos route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route covers 42.8 km with a moderate difficulty. You can expect rolling hills and open countryside typical of the Palencia province. It features paved surfaces, suitable for a racebike, with some elevation changes totaling around 347 meters up and down.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through charming villages and offers glimpses of local heritage. You'll pass by the Church of San Juan Bautista of Valdeolmillos and the Church of Santa Columba of Villamediana, both religious buildings that reflect the region's history.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete, covering a distance of 42.8 kilometers. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed and any stops you make.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route involves some elevation gain (around 347 meters) over 42.8 km. While not extremely challenging, beginners should have a reasonable level of fitness and experience with road cycling to comfortably enjoy it. There are no specific details indicating it's an advanced route, but it's not entirely flat either.

faqs.question: What is 'Monte Rey' in the context of this route?
faqs.answer: While the specific 'Monte Rey' road cycling trail is not widely documented, it likely refers to a local landmark or a specific section of the route near Villamediana and Valdeolmillos. The name suggests a hill or elevated area, contributing to the route's moderate elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through small villages like Valdeolmillos and Villamediana. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the route are not detailed, these villages may offer local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal. It's advisable to carry your own water and snacks, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding weather and clothing for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the open countryside of Palencia, you should be prepared for varying weather conditions. In spring and autumn, layers are recommended. During summer, protect yourself from the sun and carry plenty of water. Always check the local forecast for Valdeolmillos or Villamediana before heading out and dress appropriately for road cycling.
